Name: Arcanine
Flavor-Text: This legendary Chinese Pokémon is considered magnificent. Many people are enchanted by its grand mane.

Name: Azumarill
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When it plays in water, it rolls up its elongated ears to prevent their insides from getting wet.

Name: Clefable
Effects: Return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
Flavor-Text: With its acute hearing, it can pick up sounds from far away. It usually hides in quiet places.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

Name: Hitmontop
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Hitmontop by attacks is increased by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

Name: Jumpluff
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s).
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Jumpluff by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Once it catches the wind, it deftly controls its cotton-puff spores—it can even float around the world.

Name: Ninetales
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [R] Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards. This power can’t be used if Ninetales is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: Its nine beautiful tails are filled with a wondrous energy that could keep it alive for 1,000 years.

Name: Noctowl
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card. This power can’t be used if Noctowl is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes are specially adapted. They concentrate even faint light and enable it to see in the dark.

Name: Quagsire
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponetn’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Due to its relaxed and carefree attitude, it often bumps its head on boulders and boat hulls as it swims.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Discard all Energy attached to Raichu.
Flavor-Text: If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.

Name: Shuckle
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Shuckle is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to Shuckle by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The berries it stores in its vase-like shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.

Name: Slowking
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 3 cards of that player’s deck and put them back on top of that player’s deck in any order. This power can’t be used if Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.

Name: Wobbuffet
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on Wobbuffet.
Flavor-Text: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to build up its counterstrike.

Name: Ampharos
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If tails, Ampharos does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The tail’s tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

Name: Ariados
Effects: Remove from Ariados the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It’s hard to tell which end is which.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Pokémon.
Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Water-repellent powder on its wings enables it to collect honey, even in the heaviest of rains.

Name: Cleffa
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Cleffa is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Cleffa by attacks.
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 6 cards. Cleffa is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to Exeggutor.
Flip a coin for each Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: If a head drops off, it emits a telepathic call in search of others to form an Exeggcute cluster.

Name: Farfetch’d
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Switch Farfetch’d with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it eats the plant stalk it carries as emergency rations, it runs off in search of a new stalk.

Name: Feraligatr
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

Name: Furret
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It makes a nest to suit its long and skinny body. The nest is impossible for other Pokémon to enter.

Name: Granbull
Effects: Granbull does 20 damage to itself. Switch Granbull with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Granbull.
Flavor-Text: Because its fangs are too heavy, it always keeps its head tilted down. However, its bite is powerful.

Name: Hypno
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. This power can’t be used if Hypno is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Always holding a pendulum that it swings at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They have gentle hearts. Because they rarely fight, many have been caught. Their number has dwindled.

Name: Ledian
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The spot patterns on its back grow larger or smaller depending on the number of stars in the night sky.

Name: Meganium
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Remove 2 damage counters from Meganium.
Flavor-Text: Meganium’s breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

Name: Persian
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard a card from your opponent’s hand without looking.
If Persian has any [D] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its lithe muscles allow it to walk without making a sound. It attacks in an instant.

Name: Pichu
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Pichu is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Pichu by attacks.
Effects: Each player may search his or her deck for as many Basic Pokémon as he or she likes, put them onto his or her Bench, and shuffle his or her deck afterward. (You put your Pokémon on the Bench first.) Pichu is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Despite its small size, it can zap even adult humans. However, if it does so, it also surprises itself.

Name: Sandslash
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: If it digs at an incredible pace, it may snap off its spike and claws. They grow back in a day.

Name: Smoochum
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Smoochum is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Smoochum by attacks.
Effects: Move an Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon to another of your opponent’s Pokémon. Smoochum is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its lips are the most sensitive part of its body. It always uses its lips first to examine things.

Name: Sunflora
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a [G] Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Sunflora is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: It converts sunlight into energy. In the darkness after sunset, it closes its petals and becomes still.

Name: Typhlosion
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to Typhlosion.
Flavor-Text: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

Name: Tyrogue
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Tyrogue is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Tyrogue by attacks.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance. Tyrogue is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Even though it is small, it can’t be ignored because it will slug any handy target without warning.

Name: Weezing
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Weezing does 90 damage to itself, and don’t apply Weakness to this damage.
Flavor-Text: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

Name: Bayleef
Flavor-Text: A spicy aroma emanates from around its neck. The aroma acts as a stimulant to restore health.

Name: Blissey
Effects: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Blissey.
Blissey does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Anyone who takes even one taste of Blissey’s egg becomes unfailingly caring and pleasant to everyone.

Name: Corsola
Effects: Discard a [W] Energy attached to Corsola and remove all damage counters from Corsola.
Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It continuously sheds and grows. The tip of its head is prized as a treasure because of its beauty.

Name: Croconaw
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

Name: Delibird
Effects: Draw a card for each [W] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It nests at the edge of sharp cliffs. It spends all day carrying food to its awaiting chicks.

Name: Donphan
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its tackle is strong enough to knock down a house.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Return Dunsparce and all cards attached to it to your hand.
Flavor-Text: When spotted, this Pokémon escapes backward by furiously boring into the ground with its tail.

Name: Flaaffy
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won’t grow.

Name: Heracross
Effects: Draw a card for each of your [G] Pokémon in play.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This powerful Pokémon thrusts its prized horn under its enemies’ bellies, then lifts and throws them.

Name: Igglybuff
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Igglybuff is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Igglybuff by attacks.
Effects: Igglybuff is now Asleep. During your opponent’s next turn, the attack cost of each of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks is [C] more.
Flavor-Text: Its extremely flexible and elastic body makes it bounce continuously—anytime, anywhere.

Name: Mantine
Effects: Search your deck for a [W] Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Mantine can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: As it majestically swims, it doesn’t care if Remoraid attach to it for scavenging its leftovers.

Name: Metapod
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each of your [G] Pokémon has no Weakness.
Flavor-Text: It prepares for evolution by hardening its shell as much as possible to protect its soft body.

Name: Miltank
Effects: Search your deck for a Moomoo Milk card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: If it is around babies, the milk it produces contains much more nutrition than usual.

Name: Parasect
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Remove 4 damage counters from Parasect.
Flavor-Text: The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.

Name: Quilava
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to Quilava.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is fully covered by nonflammable fur. It can withstand any kind of fire attack.

Name: Qwilfish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: To fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of water all at once.

Name: Skiploom
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The bloom on top of its head opens and closes as the temperature fluctuates up and down.

Name: Slowbro
Effects: Both Slowbro and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.
Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each [P] Energy attached to Slowbro.
Flavor-Text: If the tail-biting Shellder is thrown off in a harsh battle, it reverts to being an ordinary Slowpoke.

Name: Starmie
Effects: Choose as many [W] Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy you chose. Shuffle those cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: The middle section of its body is called the core. It glows in a different color each time it is seen.

Name: Unown
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Unown from your hand onto your Bench, you may return all Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to your hand.
Flavor-Text: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

Name: Unown
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Unown from your hand onto your Bench, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
Flavor-Text: Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Wigglytuff by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a Body Slam.

Name: Caterpie
Flavor-Text: Its feet have suction cups designed to stick to any surface. It tenaciously climbs trees to forage.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 3 damage counters from Chansey.
Flavor-Text: It walks carefully to prevent its egg from breaking. However, it is extremely fast at running away.

Name: Chikorita
Flavor-Text: Its pleasantly aromatic leaf has the ability to check the humidity and temperature.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Clefairy by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.

Name: Cyndaquil
Flavor-Text: It usually stays hunched over. If it is angry or surprised, it shoots flames out of its back.

Name: Drowzee
Effects: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children’s are much tastier.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Using telepathy only they can employ, they always form a cluster of six Exeggcute.

Name: Girafarig
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent and bite.

Name: Growlithe
Flavor-Text: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

Name: Hoothoot
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

Name: Hoppip
Effects: You may switch Hoppip with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: To keep from being blown away by the wind, they gather in clusters. But they do enjoy gentle breezes.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Looking into its cute, round eyes causes it to sing a relaxing melody, inducing its enemies to sleep.

Name: Jynx
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent’s hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It rocks its body rhythmically. It appears to alter the rhythm depending on how it is feeling.

Name: Koffing
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its thin, filmy body is filled with gases that cause constant sniffles, coughs and teary eyes.

Name: Ledyba
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When the weather turns cold, lots of Ledyba gather from everywhere to cluster and keep each other warm.

Name: Magikarp
Flavor-Text: For no reason, it jumps and splashes about, making it easy for predators like Pidgeotto to catch it mid-jump.

Name: Mareep
Effects: Search your deck for a number of [L] Energy cards up to the number of Mareep in play (both yours and your opponent’s) and attach them to Mareep. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: If static electricity builds in its body, its fleece doubles in volume. Touching it will shock you.

Name: Marill
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The end of its tail serves as a buoy that keeps it from drowning, even in a vicious current.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It loves anything that shines. It especially adores coins that it picks up and secretly hoards.

Name: Paras
Effects: Paras does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: As its body grows, large mushrooms named tochukaso start sprouting out of its back.

Name: Phanpy
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Phanpy.
Flavor-Text: It swings its long snout around playfully, but because it is so strong, that can be dangerous.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

Name: Sandshrew
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Sandshrew by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Disliking water, it lives in deep burrows in arid areas. It can roll itself instantly into a ball.

Name: Sentret
Effects: Look at your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: A very cautious Pokémon, it raises itself up using its tail to get a better view of its surroundings.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won’t even notice for a whole day.

Name: Snubbull
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has an active, playful nature. Many women like to frolic with it because of its affectionate ways.

Name: Spinarak
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

Name: Staryu
Flavor-Text: At night, the center of its body slowly flickers with the same rhythm as a human heartbeat.

Name: Sunkern
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It may plummet from the sky. If attacked by a Spearow, it will violently shake its leaves.

Name: Totodile
Flavor-Text: Its powerful, well-developed jaws are capable of crushing anything. Even its trainer must be careful.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to Vulpix.
Flavor-Text: If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.

Name: Wooper
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: When it walks around on the ground, it coats its body with a slimy, poisonous film.

Name: Bill
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Copycat
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent’s hand.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a basic Energy card attached 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

Name: Fisherman
Description: Search your discard pile for 4 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Full Heal
Description: Remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.

Name: Moomoo Milk
Description: Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Flip 2 coins. For each heads, remove 3 damage counters from that Pokémon.

Name: Poké Ball
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Pokégear 3.0
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. Choose a Supporter card you find there, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

Name: Pokémon Collector
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Pokémon Communication
Description: Choose 1 Pokémon in your hand, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. If you do, search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Pokémon Reversal
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon, and switch it with your opponent’s Active Pokémon.

Name: Professor Elm’s Training Method
Description: Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Professor Oak’s New Theory
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Switch
Description: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Rainbow Energy
Description: Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. (While not in play, Rainbow Energy counts as [C] Energy.)

Name: Ampharos
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage. If tails, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Blissey
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Blissey from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may remove all damage counters from all of your Pokémon. If you do, discard all Energy attached to those Pokémon that had any damage counters on them.

Name: Donphan
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Any damage done to Donphan by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Feraligatr
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your [W] Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Feraligatr is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Meganium
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [G] Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Meganium is affected by a Special Condition.

Name: Typhlosion
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a [R] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Typhlosion is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard an Energy card attached to Typhlosion and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Ho-Oh LEGEND
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: All Energy attached to Ho-Oh LEGEND are [R] Energy instead of their usual type.
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to Ho-Oh LEGEND.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Ho-Oh LEGEND.
Flavor-Text: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world’s skies continuously on its magnificent, seven-colored wings.

Name: Ho-Oh LEGEND
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: All Energy attached to Ho-Oh LEGEND are [R] Energy instead of their usual type.
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to Ho-Oh LEGEND.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Ho-Oh LEGEND.
Flavor-Text: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world’s skies continuously on its magnificent, seven-colored wings.

Name: Lugia LEGEND
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Lugia LEGEND into play, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck and attach all Energy cards you find there to Lugia LEGEND. Discard the other cards.
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy, [W] Energy, and [L] Energy attached to Lugia LEGEND.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Lugia LEGEND.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

Name: Lugia LEGEND
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Lugia LEGEND into play, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck and attach all Energy cards you find there to Lugia LEGEND. Discard the other cards.
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy, [W] Energy, and [L] Energy attached to Lugia LEGEND.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Lugia LEGEND.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, Gyarados does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They say that during past strife, Gyarados would appear and leave blazing ruins in its wake.
